description:
GOAL: Make GC actually run from JIT'd code. Currently `gc()` is invoked
only from Rust tests (search ir_codegen.rs: every gc() call site is in
test code). JIT'd code allocates indefinitely without collecting. This
ticket bundles two tightly-coupled pieces — safepoint placement AND
the frame-chain root walker — because they are useless apart.

This ticket also SUBSUMES fz-ul4.11.30 (wire ir_liveness or delete).
The root walker needs per-frame "which slots carry live FzValues"
information; ir_liveness::analyze_module is the half-built source of
that information. This ticket makes the call: either consume
ir_liveness or replace it with a leaner per-fn FzValue-slot computation
done inside build_frame_schema.

## Decisions captured (user, 2026-05-11)

- Safepoint strategy: HYBRID (option 1d).
  - Trampoline-tick check is primary. Before each frame dispatch in
    `ir_codegen.rs:124-154`, check the current process's heap
    occupancy. If over threshold, run GC before reading the next
    schema_id and calling the next fn.
  - Alloc-edge bumps a soft "over-threshold" flag on the heap. Each
    `fz_alloc_*` runtime fn sets the flag when occupancy crosses
    threshold during that allocation. Trampoline reads + clears the
    flag at the next tick. No GC during the FFI fn itself — keeps
    allocations re-entrance-safe.
- Root-walking strategy: SCHEMA SLOT-KIND (option 2a) with cont_ptr
  AT A FIXED OFFSET (not a schema variant).
  - `FieldKind` already has `FzValue` and `RawBytes(u32)`. KEEP AS-IS.
  - cont_ptr lives at `frame[16]` (header_size offset; see
    ir_codegen.rs:129). It is not a schema slot; the walker reads it
    structurally.
  - Walker starts at the current frame ptr (held by trampoline /
    Process post-.11.32). For each frame: read schema; iterate
    FieldDescriptors; emit FzValue slots into the root set. Chain to
    next frame via `frame[16]`. Halt when frame[16] is null.

## Why bundled, not split

Safepoints with no root walker means gc(roots=[]) frees everything.
A walker with no safepoint has no consumer. The trampoline-tick path
is the only safepoint; the walker is its only valid root source. One
commit.

## Why we subsume .11.30

.11.30's question — "wire ir_liveness or delete it?" — is exactly the
question .11.31 must answer to build its root walker. ir_liveness
already computes "FzValue slots carried across calls" per FnIr. Two
clean paths:

A. Consume ir_liveness::analyze_module as the FieldKind source.
   build_frame_schema uses analyze_module's output to tag each slot
   FzValue or RawBytes correctly.

B. Inline a simpler per-fn FzValue-slot computation inside
   build_frame_schema (currently uses entry_block.params.len() and
   tags everything FzValue by default). The richer per-call analysis
   in ir_liveness becomes dead.

DECIDE at impl time. Lean toward (A): the analysis exists and is
correct; deleting it just to re-implement leaner is anti-progress
unless the leaner version is materially simpler. Either way, .11.30
closes as "subsumed by .11.31" no matter which sub-option lands.

## Deliverables

### A. Frame-chain root walker (src/heap.rs or new src/gc_roots.rs)

```rust
pub fn collect_roots_from_frame_chain(
    cur: *mut HeapHeader,
    schemas: &SchemaRegistry,
) -> Vec<FzValue>;
```

Walks `cur` backward via frame[16] (cont_ptr). For each frame: read
schema, emit FzValue slots. Returns root set. Guard with visited-set
for cycle safety (structural cycles shouldn't exist but cheap to add).

### B. Schema correctness audit

Audit every schema producer:
- `build_frame_schema` (ir_codegen.rs; line was 1454-1468, post-.11.25
  may have shifted): currently tags slots by entry-param count;
  verify each user-Var slot is correctly FzValue or RawBytes via the
  ir_liveness output (if (A)) or local computation (if (B)).
- Tuple / struct / closure / list cons / bitstring / map / vec*
  schemas (heap.rs / ir_codegen.rs scattered): verify FieldKind tags.
  Specifically Vec{I64,F64,U8,Bit} payloads must be RawBytes; Float
  box payload RawBytes; Closure captured slots FzValue; List cons
  head + tail FzValue; Map key/value FzValue; etc.

### C. Trampoline integration (src/ir_codegen.rs:124-154)

Depends on .11.32 having landed (Process holds heap). Post-.11.32:

```rust
loop {
    if cur.is_null() { break; }
    if process.heap.should_gc() {
        let roots = collect_roots_from_frame_chain(cur, &process.heap.schemas);
        process.heap.gc(&roots);
        process.heap.clear_should_gc_flag();
    }
    // existing dispatch...
}
```

If .11.31 lands BEFORE .11.32, use TLS HEAP during the gap; rewrite
in .11.32.

### D. Alloc-edge over-threshold flag (src/heap.rs)

```rust
pub struct Heap {
    // existing fields...
    over_threshold: Cell<bool>,
    gc_threshold_bytes: usize,    // configurable; default e.g. 1MiB
}

impl Heap {
    fn note_alloc(&self, new_used: usize) {
        if new_used >= self.gc_threshold_bytes {
            self.over_threshold.set(true);
        }
    }
    pub fn should_gc(&self) -> bool { self.over_threshold.get() }
    pub fn clear_should_gc_flag(&self) { self.over_threshold.set(false); }
}
```

Wire `note_alloc` at the bottom of `Heap::alloc` and friends.

## Tests (TDD order)

1. Root walker on a 3-frame chain with known schemas returns the
   expected FzValue set.
2. Walker safely handles a chain where one frame has a RawBytes-only
   schema (no roots from it).
3. Walker terminates on null cont_ptr (top of stack).
4. Alloc that crosses threshold sets the over_threshold flag.
5. Manual trampoline run that allocates past threshold, asserts GC
   was invoked (instrument gc() with a per-Heap gc_run_count counter
   incremented on entry).
6. Hot-loop allocation pattern: 1000 iterations, each allocating a
   list cons cell, with the only live reference in the entry frame.
   After full run, live_count is small (only the survivor list);
   gc_run_count >= 1.
7. Slot-kind correctness regression: a frame whose schema mis-tags a
   raw int slot as FzValue is detectable (the walker emits the int as
   a "value" — value should panic on tag decode, OR the walker should
   sanity-check the tag bits and skip non-pointer values). Decide at
   impl time: strict (panic on bad tag) or permissive (skip).
8. SAMPLE-based tests still pass.

## Risks

- Schema mis-tagging causes under-marking (use-after-free) or
  over-marking (memory waste + spurious retention). Mitigation:
  audit + one test per HeapKind.
- ir_liveness output may not handle all the schema producers we need.
  Mitigation: identify gap during audit; either extend ir_liveness or
  switch to plan (B).
- Cell<bool> is single-threaded; under multi-thread scheduler
  (post-.19.1) becomes AtomicBool. Documented.

## Out of scope

- Generational nursery (follow-up).
- Concurrent / incremental GC (follow-up).
- Write barriers (no mutation in fz).

## References

- src/heap.rs:73-96 (Heap struct)
- src/heap.rs (current gc() entry + trace walker)
- src/heap.rs:25-28 (FieldKind — staying as-is)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:124-154 (trampoline)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:129 (cont_ptr at frame[16])
- src/ir_liveness.rs (analyze_module — the half-built source)
- fz-ul4.11.32 (per-Process FFI ctx — pairs)
- fz-ul4.11.30 (SUBSUMED — close on commit of .11.31)

## Acceptance

- `gc_run_count` increases under hot allocation in JIT.
- Slot-kind correctness verified per HeapKind.
- No regressions in existing tests.
- fz-ul4.11.30 closed as "subsumed by .11.31".
- One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.31.
title: Real GC: trampoline-tick safepoints + frame-chain root walker
